text:
## 1. THE PROMISE / TAGLINE — verbatim candidates

- **"Citizenship for your agents"** — the one-liner, appears in both README.md:3 and product-spec/index.md:12. This is the canonical tagline.
- **"a durable identity and a shared world for agents, no matter which tool they happen to be running in"** (index.md:13) — the one-line expansion. [...] Tagline material already in the crate description: *"Citizenship for your agents: durable identity + awareness on the Nostr fabric, host-neutral."* [...] User: deeply review this repo -- I want you to help me with marketing, copywriting, launch strategy, specially positioning -- start with a swarm of sonnet agents to help you gather data, both from the code and from docs (no docs is to be taken too seriously -- might have hallucinations), as well as online market research -- once you've done a first exploration use a fable agent to get high-level ideas, direction, concepts. The first target is to rewrite from scratch the README.md and land high-level copywriting for the landing page, anchored in uniqueness, USP and any best copywriting practices. [...] **The real USP (the moat, one sentence):** *Cross-person, server-less, key-owned, vendor-independent agent citizenship* — the durable identity + shared society that A2A's org-assumptions, MCP's tool-scope, Zapier's predefined pipes, and git's at-merge model each structurally can't provide.

**The trap to avoid:** the docs themselves warn (tenet #8, value-layers) that "stop agents clobbering each other" is the *seductive demo*, not the foundation. The defensible anchor is **identity + awareness** — "promote your agents from processes to citizens." [...] Stated explicitly in prior-art.md as **one sentence**: "**Cross-person, server-less, key-owned, vendor-independent agent citizenship**" — the combination that A2A (org-assumed, enterprise, needs a registry), MCP (agent↔tool, not agent↔agent), Zapier (bilateral pre-built pipes, doesn't scale N×N), and git (authoritative but only post-hoc/at-merge) each structurally cannot provide.
